name: spiderfoot
title: spiderfoot
description:
OSINT collection and reconnaissance tool
This package contains an open source intelligence (OSINT) automation tool. Its
goal is to automate the process of gathering intelligence about a given
target, which may be an IP address, domain name, hostname, network subnet,
ASN, e-mail address or person's name.
.
SpiderFoot can be used offensively, i.e. as part of a black-box penetration
test to gather information about the target, or defensively to identify what
information you or your organisation are freely providing for attackers to use
against you.

commands.name: spiderfoot-cli
commands.description: usage: sfcli.py [-h] [-d] [-s URL] [-u USER] [-p PASS] [-P PASSFILE] [-e FILE]

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use spiderfoot because OSINT collection and reconnaissance tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use spiderfoot as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `spiderfoot [options] [args]` as documented upstream for spiderfoot.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair spiderfoot with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `spiderfoot --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
